questions.q: Brahmagupta is best remembered for being one of the first to set out rules for arithmetic involving which number?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta gave the earliest known systematic rules for arithmetic with zero, earning him the nickname 'Master of Zero'.

questions.q: In which century did Brahmagupta live and work?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta was a 7th-century mathematician and astronomer, born around 598 CE.

questions.q: What was Brahmagupta's primary profession besides mathematics?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta was an astronomer who headed an observatory and wrote major works on astronomy as well as mathematics.

questions.q: What is the title of Brahmagupta's most famous treatise?
questions.explanation: The Brahmasphutasiddhanta ('Correctly Established Doctrine of Brahma'), written in 628 CE, is Brahmagupta's landmark work.

questions.q: Brahmagupta was among the first to formulate rules for arithmetic with which kind of numbers, besides zero?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta gave systematic rules for operating with negative numbers, which he described using the language of debts and fortunes.

questions.q: Brahmagupta is celebrated as a great figure in the history of which country's mathematics?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta was an Indian mathematician and astronomer, a central figure in classical Indian mathematics.

questions.q: According to Brahmagupta's rules, what is the result of adding zero to a number?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta correctly stated that a number remains unchanged when zero is added to it.

questions.q: Brahmagupta described positive and negative numbers using which everyday concepts?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta framed positive quantities as 'fortunes' and negative quantities as 'debts', a practical way to explain signed arithmetic.

questions.q: In what language was Brahmagupta's Brahmasphutasiddhanta originally written?
questions.explanation: Like most classical Indian scientific works, the Brahmasphutasiddhanta was composed in Sanskrit, largely in verse.

questions.q: Brahmagupta's work primarily belongs to which broad fields of study?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta made major contributions to both mathematics and astronomy, the two fields his treatises cover.

questions.q: In which year was the Brahmasphutasiddhanta composed?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta completed the Brahmasphutasiddhanta in 628 CE, when he was about 30 years old.

questions.q: Brahmagupta is traditionally associated with working in which region of India?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta lived in Bhillamala (modern Bhinmal, Rajasthan); he later also worked at the astronomical center of Ujjain.

questions.q: Brahmagupta's famous formula gives the area of which kind of figure?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta's formula computes the area of a cyclic quadrilateral from its four side lengths.

questions.q: Brahmagupta's formula for a cyclic quadrilateral with sides a, b, c, d and semiperimeter s gives the area as:
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta's formula is Area = √((s−a)(s−b)(s−c)(s−d)), generalizing Heron's formula for triangles.

questions.q: Brahmagupta's formula is often described as a generalization of which earlier result?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta's formula reduces to Heron's formula when one side of the quadrilateral shrinks to zero, leaving a triangle.

questions.q: What did Brahmagupta correctly state about the product of two negative numbers?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta stated that the product (or quotient) of two negatives is positive, while a positive times a negative is negative.

questions.q: Which of these statements about division by zero did Brahmagupta attempt, even though it was not fully correct by modern standards?
options: He proved division by zero is impossible
He gave rules for dividing a number by zero
He defined zero divided by zero as one
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta tried to give rules for arithmetic involving division by zero, an early but imperfect attempt later refined by Bhaskara II.

questions.q: Brahmagupta worked on solving which type of equation, now often named after a European mathematician?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta studied the equation Nx² + 1 = y², the so-called Pell's equation, providing methods to generate solutions.

questions.q: Brahmagupta's identity concerns the product of two numbers, each of which is a sum involving squares of what form?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta's identity shows that the product of two numbers of the form x² + Ny² is again of that same form.

questions.q: Roughly when was Brahmagupta born?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta was born around 598 CE, placing his career in the early-to-mid 7th century.

questions.q: The Brahmasphutasiddhanta also contained early work in which branch of mathematics dealing with unknowns?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta advanced algebra, including methods for solving linear and quadratic equations and handling unknowns.

questions.q: Brahmagupta gave a general solution method for which type of equation in one unknown?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta provided a general rule equivalent to the quadratic formula for solving quadratic equations.

questions.q: How did the Brahmasphutasiddhanta influence mathematics in the wider medieval world?
options: It was translated into Arabic and helped spread Indian numerals and zero
It was lost until the 19th century
It was never translated outside India
It was translated only into Chinese
questions.explanation: The work was translated into Arabic in the 8th century (notably under al-Mansur), helping transmit the decimal system and zero to the Islamic world and later Europe.

questions.q: Which later Indian mathematician built on Brahmagupta's work and is also celebrated for treatment of zero and Pell's equation?
questions.explanation: Bhaskara II, in the 12th century, extended Brahmagupta's results on zero, negative numbers, and the equation Nx² + 1 = y².

questions.q: Brahmagupta's later astronomical work, the Khandakhadyaka, was written approximately when?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta composed the Khandakhadyaka, a practical astronomical handbook, around 665 CE, decades after the Brahmasphutasiddhanta.

questions.q: What is one error in Brahmagupta's rules for zero, as judged by modern mathematics?
options: He said adding zero changes a number
He effectively claimed zero divided by zero equals zero
He said zero times a number is the number
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta stated that zero divided by zero is zero, which modern mathematics regards as undefined (indeterminate).

questions.q: Brahmagupta is associated with which special class of cyclic quadrilaterals named in his honor?
questions.explanation: A Brahmagupta quadrilateral is a cyclic quadrilateral with perpendicular diagonals and rational sides, diagonals, and area.

questions.q: Beyond pure mathematics, Brahmagupta also discussed which physical idea in his astronomical writings?
options: Gravity as a force of attraction toward the Earth
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta described a notion of gravity, stating that bodies fall toward the Earth because it is the Earth's nature to attract them.

questions.q: Brahmagupta's chord/sine computations and interpolation methods contributed to which area of mathematics used in astronomy?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta produced sine tables and an interpolation formula, advancing the trigonometry needed for astronomical calculation.

questions.q: Which method, important for solving Pell-type equations, is attributed in part to Brahmagupta and later perfected by Bhaskara II?
questions.explanation: Brahmagupta laid groundwork for the chakravala (cyclic) method for Nx² + 1 = y², which Bhaskara II later completed.
